seo.meta_title: Supabase Auth vs Logto for Next.js App Router (2026): Which Auth Tool Wins? (Updated Week 24, 2026)
seo.meta_description: Detailed Supabase Auth vs Logto comparison for Next.js App Router. See how they stack up on pricing, developer experience, and real-world trade-offs.
seo.og_title: Supabase Auth or Logto: What Actually Matters for Next.js
seo.og_description: Detailed comparison of Supabase Auth vs Logto for Next.js App Router. Pricing breakdown, real DX scores, and which one I'd recommend for your 2026 project.

hero.headline: Supabase Auth vs Logto for Next.js Authentication
hero.subheadline: An honest comparison of Supabase Auth and Logto — pricing, DX, setup time, and which one we recommend for Next.js App Router projects in 2026.

quick_summary: Both Supabase Auth and Logto are solid choices for Next.js authentication, but they serve different needs. Supabase Auth edges ahead with a stronger Next.js integration score (7/10 vs 7/10). Where Logto shines is Yes, up to 5,000 MAU. If you are optimizing for developer experience in the App Router, Supabase Auth is the safer bet.
verdict_text: We recommend Supabase Auth for most Next.js App Router projects. Its easy learning curve and 15 minutes setup time make it immediately productive. Logto is a worthy alternative, particularly if you need yes, up to 5,000 mau or prefer Logto's approach to authentication.

structured_data_points.label: Free Tier
structured_data_points.t1: Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier

pros: Extremely generous free tier with 50,000 monthly active users
Integrated with Supabase database, storage, and realtime subscriptions
Built-in support for email, phone, OAuth, and magic links
cons: Tightly coupled to the Supabase PostgreSQL ecosystem
Less focused on auth-only use cases - it's part of a larger platform
Organization and team management features are less mature than Clerk
RLS policies can become complex to manage at scale

pros: Built-in management API and admin console
Support for social sign-in, MFA, and passwordless auth
User-friendly admin dashboard for non-technical team members
cons: Smaller community and fewer third-party integrations
Next.js SDK is less mature with fewer examples
Self-hosted version requires significant infrastructure setup

faq.question: Should I choose Supabase Auth or Logto for my Next.js app?
faq.answer: It depends on your specific needs. Supabase Auth scores 7/10 for Next.js integration with a Easy learning curve. Logto scores 7/10 with a Medium learning curve. For most projects, we recommend Supabase Auth because of its stronger DX and better alignment with App Router patterns. However, if Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier or Yes, up to 5,000 MAU is a deciding factor, the cheaper option may serve you well.

faq.question: Is Supabase Auth or Logto more affordable?
faq.answer: Supabase Auth starts at Free – $75/mo with a free tier of Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier. Logto starts at Free – $599/mo with a free tier of Yes, up to 5,000 MAU. The total cost of ownership depends on your MAU (monthly active users). At lower volumes, the free tiers of both are sufficient. As you scale, Supabase Auth offers better value at scale. Factor in hidden costs like enterprise features, SSO, and database infrastructure when comparing.

faq.question: Is Supabase Auth or Logto easier to set up with Next.js?
faq.answer: Supabase Auth has a Easy learning curve with an estimated setup time of 15 minutes. Logto has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 20 minutes. Supabase Auth is faster to set up, but ease of integration also depends on your team's familiarity with the framework and whether you want pre-built UI components or prefer to build your own.

faq.question: Can I use Supabase Auth and Logto together?
faq.answer: Using both Supabase Auth and Logto in the same Next.js application is not recommended. They would create separate session management systems, conflicting middleware, and competing cookies. Choose one auth provider and stick with it. Migrating between auth providers later is possible but requires user data export and rebuilding authentication flows.

faq.question: Which has better developer experience: Supabase Auth or Logto?
faq.answer: Supabase Auth has a Easy learning curve, a Next.js integration score of 7/10, and a user rating of 4.4★. Logto has a Medium learning curve, 7/10, and 4.1★. On paper, Supabase Auth leads in DX. In practice, your specific requirements around pre-built components, customization, and team expertise matter just as much. Try both free tiers to see what clicks.
